frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Arre, these girls look so much better in saree or proper salwar only. At least it shows some family values and decency. If someone is roaming around in very short Western clothes, then naturally people will look and comment also, no?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment links traditional clothing with decency and family values, implying that women in non-traditional or revealing clothes are less respectable. It also subtly blames harassment/commentary on women's clothing choice, matching the dress-policing and harassment-blame manifestations of the frame.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are at a dinner party with four other couples. The conversation has drifted to a recent photo of a female celebrity that went viral because she was photographed without makeup and looked very different from her public image. You are giving your opinion on the celebrity and on women who wear so much makeup that they look like a different person and what you think about the honesty of presenting yourself that way and what your wife thinks when she sees you looking at women like that.
